all.en: On New Year's Day, I bought a plain blue notebook from a small stationery shop.
all.zh: 元旦那天，我從一家小文具店買了一本素面藍色筆記本。

all.en: My goal was simple: write at least one page every single morning for a year.
all.zh: 我的目標很簡單：整整一年，每天早晨都寫至少一頁。

all.en: I had tried journaling before, but I usually gave up before the second week.
all.zh: 我以前也試過寫日記，但通常還沒撐到第二週就放棄了。

all.en: This time, I decided to make the habit ridiculously easy to start each day.
all.zh: 這次，我決定讓開始這個習慣變得簡單到不可思議。

all.en: The pen and notebook stayed on my kitchen table, right next to the kettle.
all.zh: 筆和筆記本就放在廚房桌上，緊鄰著熱水壺。

all.en: While the water boiled for tea, I forced myself to write the date down first.
all.zh: 等熱水煮開的時候，我會強迫自己先寫下日期。

all.en: Some mornings I only wrote three sentences, and that was perfectly acceptable too.
all.zh: 有些早晨我只寫了三句話，那樣也完全可以接受。

all.en: Other mornings the words poured out and filled four full pages without effort.
all.zh: 有些早晨字句滔滔不絕，毫不費力地寫滿了整整四頁。

all.en: The first month felt awkward, like I was talking to a stranger about my life.
all.zh: 第一個月感覺很彆扭，好像在跟陌生人講述我的人生。

all.en: By February, the notebook started to feel like a quiet friend who never interrupted.
all.zh: 到了二月，這本筆記本開始像一位從不打斷我的安靜朋友。

all.en: I noticed I kept writing about the same coworker every Monday morning without fail.
all.zh: 我注意到自己每個週一早晨都會反覆寫到同一位同事。

all.en: Reading those pages back showed me a problem I had been ignoring for months.
all.zh: 回頭讀那些頁面讓我看見一個我已經忽視好幾個月的問題。

all.en: After a long talk with my manager, the situation at work slowly began to improve.
all.zh: 和主管長談之後，工作上的狀況開始慢慢好轉。

all.en: Without the journal, I might never have realized how much that issue affected me.
all.zh: 如果沒有這本日記，我可能永遠不會發現那件事對我影響有多大。

all.en: In March, I started ending each entry with three small things I was grateful for.
all.zh: 三月時，我開始在每篇日記的結尾寫下三件讓我感激的小事。

all.en: Some entries listed warm bread, sunlight on the floor, or a kind email from my aunt.
all.zh: 有些條目寫的是溫熱的麵包、灑在地板上的陽光，或是阿姨寄來的溫暖郵件。

all.en: These tiny notes shifted how I noticed the world during the rest of the day.
all.zh: 這些小小的紀錄改變了我在一天當中其餘時間看世界的方式。

all.en: In April, I tried writing my dreams down whenever I could remember them clearly.
all.zh: 四月時，每當我清晰記得夢境，就試著把它寫下來。

all.en: Many of my dreams included old classmates I had not seen in over a decade.
all.zh: 我的夢裡常出現十多年沒見的老同學。

all.en: I reached out to two of them, and we had dinner together a month later.
all.zh: 我聯絡了其中兩位，一個月後我們一起吃了晚餐。

all.en: Summer brought difficult entries about my father's worsening health and frequent hospital visits.
all.zh: 夏天帶來了沉重的條目，關於父親每況愈下的健康，以及頻繁的醫院往返。

all.en: Writing those pages was painful, but it also kept me from carrying everything alone.
all.zh: 寫那些頁面很痛苦，但也讓我不必獨自扛起一切。

all.en: I cried on certain mornings, and the tears would smudge the ink into small clouds.
all.zh: 有幾個早晨我哭了，眼淚把墨水暈成一朵朵小雲。

all.en: I kept those pages exactly as they were, with no attempt to clean them up.
all.zh: 我把那些頁面原封不動地留著，沒有想過要把它們擦乾淨。

all.en: By autumn, my handwriting had loosened and my sentences had grown more honest.
all.zh: 到了秋天，我的字跡變得更放鬆，句子也變得更真誠。

all.en: I stopped writing what I thought I should feel and started writing what I actually felt.
all.zh: 我不再寫自己以為應該有的感受，開始寫自己真實的感受。

all.en: That single change brought a kind of inner quiet I had been searching for for years.
all.zh: 光是這一個改變，就帶來了我多年來一直在尋找的內在平靜。

all.en: In November, I began rereading entries from January and laughing at my old worries.
all.zh: 十一月時，我開始重讀一月份的條目，對著當時的煩惱發笑。

all.en: Many of the problems that had felt huge then now seemed quite small and manageable.
all.zh: 許多當時感覺巨大的問題，現在看起來都相當渺小、容易處理。

all.en: That perspective itself became one of the most valuable lessons of the whole year.
all.zh: 這份視角本身，就成了整整一年中最寶貴的一課。

all.en: On December 31st, I wrote the final entry while sitting by the kitchen window.
all.zh: 十二月三十一日，我坐在廚房窗邊寫下了最後一篇日記。

all.en: I counted three hundred and sixty-five dated entries, with only two missing days.
all.zh: 我數了一下，有三百六十五篇有日期的條目，只有兩天沒寫。

all.en: The notebook had become heavy, soft at the corners, and very dear to me.
all.zh: 這本筆記本變得厚重，邊角柔軟，對我來說也變得非常珍貴。

all.en: I placed it on my bookshelf next to other quiet treasures from my life.
all.zh: 我把它放在書架上，旁邊都是我人生中其他靜默的珍寶。

all.en: Then I opened a fresh notebook, dated it January 1st, and began writing again.
all.zh: 接著我翻開一本全新的筆記本，寫上元旦的日期，又開始書寫。

all.en: In the second year, I added a small evening ritual of five lines before bed each night.
all.zh: 進入第二年後，我在每晚就寢前新增了一個寫五行字的小儀式。

all.en: Those evening notes captured tiny details that the busy morning version always missed.
all.zh: 那些夜晚的紀錄捕捉到許多忙碌晨間版本總是漏掉的小細節。

all.en: A close friend asked me how I managed to keep writing through such busy stressful weeks.
all.zh: 一位好友問我，是怎麼在如此繁忙又有壓力的幾週中持續書寫的。

all.en: I told her that journaling was less about discipline and more about a private daily kindness.
all.zh: 我告訴她，寫日記與其說是自律，不如說是給自己每日的一份私密善意。

all.en: She started her own notebook that night, and we now compare gratitude lists every Sunday.
all.zh: 她當晚就開始了自己的筆記本，現在我們每個星期天都會交換感恩清單。

all.en: My father recovered slowly during the spring, and his name began appearing in happier entries.
all.zh: 我父親在春天時慢慢康復，他的名字開始出現在較為快樂的條目中。

all.en: I read him passages I had written during his hospital stay, and he held my hand tightly.
all.zh: 我朗讀他住院期間我寫下的段落，他緊緊地握著我的手。

all.en: He asked me to keep writing forever, because the words felt like quiet medicine to him.
all.zh: 他要我永遠寫下去，因為那些文字對他而言宛如安靜的良藥。

all.en: I now keep both notebooks side by side, one for mornings and one for honest tired evenings.
all.zh: 我現在把兩本筆記本並排放著，一本寫晨間，一本寫真誠疲憊的夜晚。

all.en: Together they tell a fuller story of a life that is finally listening to itself carefully.
all.zh: 它們合在一起，訴說著一段終於開始仔細聆聽自己的人生。
